name: Drawer
description: A slide-out panel anchored to a screen edge for supplementary content, navigation, or forms. Supports swipe-to-dismiss, snap points, and nested drawers. Built on Base UI Drawer with StyleX styling.

Root.description: State container. Renders no DOM element. Provides swipe direction context to child parts.

defaultOpen.description: Initial open state for uncontrolled mode.

onOpenChange.description: Callback fired when the drawer opens or closes.

onOpenChangeComplete.description: Callback fired after open/close animations complete.

modal.description: Whether the drawer is modal (traps focus and renders backdrop).

swipeDirection.description: Direction to swipe to dismiss. Also determines which screen edge the drawer anchors to.

snapPoints.description: Array of snap points (0–1) the drawer can rest at when swiped.

Trigger.description: Button that opens the drawer. Unstyled by default — use the render prop to swap in a styled Button.

render.description: Replace the underlying element. Use to render a styled Button.

dataAttributes.data-popup-open: Present when the drawer is open.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the trigger is disabled.

Portal.description: Renders children in a React portal outside the DOM tree.

keepMounted.description: Keep in DOM when closed. Pass true if you need exit animations and handle closed-state visibility in CSS.

container.description: Target parent element for the portal.

Backdrop.description: Semi-transparent overlay behind the drawer. Clicking it closes the drawer. Has fade animation via global CSS.

dataAttributes.data-open: Present when the drawer is open.
dataAttributes.data-closed: Present when the drawer is closed.

Popup.description: The drawer panel anchored to a screen edge based on swipeDirection. Positioned fixed with rounded corners on the opening side. Includes an optional close button (X icon) in the top-right corner.

showCloseButton.description: Whether to show the X close button in the top-right corner.

initialFocus.description: Element to focus when the drawer opens.

finalFocus.description: Element to focus when the drawer closes.

dataAttributes.data-open: Present when the drawer is open.
dataAttributes.data-closed: Present when the drawer is closed.
dataAttributes.data-nested: Present when nested inside another drawer.
dataAttributes.data-nested-drawer-open: Present when a child drawer is open.
dataAttributes.data-swiping: Present while the user is actively swiping.
dataAttributes.data-swipe-direction: The configured swipe direction (up, down, left, right).
dataAttributes.data-expanded: Present when the drawer is fully expanded.

Header.description: Container for Title and Description. Provides consistent padding above the scrollable Panel. Custom part not in Base UI.

Title.description: Drawer heading. Automatically wired to aria-labelledby.

Description.description: Drawer body text below the title. Automatically wired to aria-describedby.

Panel.description: Scrollable content area between Header and Footer. Uses data attributes and inset box-shadow for scroll indicators. Custom part not in Base UI.

dataAttributes.data-scroll-top: Present when content is scrolled away from the top (more content above).
dataAttributes.data-scroll-bottom: Present when there is more content below the visible area.

Footer.description: Flex row for action buttons (Cancel/Submit/Done). Custom part not in Base UI.

variant.description: Visual style. 'default' has no top border, 'bordered' adds a separator line.

Close.description: Button that closes the drawer on click. Unstyled — use the render prop to swap in a styled Button.

render.description: Replace the underlying element. Use to render a styled Button.

cssRequirements.description: Backdrop fade and popup slide animations require global CSS rules inside @layer priority1. StyleX cannot target data-attribute selectors, so these rules must live in the consumer's global stylesheet.

intents.reasoning: Drawer is ideal for navigation menus that slide in from a screen edge, especially on mobile where horizontal space is limited.
Drawer with default bottom positioning provides a natural mobile bottom sheet pattern with swipe-to-dismiss support.
intents.composition:
<Drawer.Root>
  <Drawer.Trigger render={<Button>Share</Button>} />
  <Drawer.Portal>
    <Drawer.Backdrop />
    <Drawer.Popup>
      <Drawer.Header>
        <Drawer.Title>Share</Drawer.Title>
        <Drawer.Description>Choose how to share this content.</Drawer.Description>
      </Drawer.Header>
      <Drawer.Panel>
        {/* share options */}
      </Drawer.Panel>
    </Drawer.Popup>
  </Drawer.Portal>
</Drawer.Root>

intents.reasoning: Drawer provides a non-disruptive overlay for forms and editors that supplements the main content without fully obscuring it.
intents.composition:
<Drawer.Root swipeDirection="right" open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen}>
  <Drawer.Trigger render={<Button>Edit</Button>} />
  <Drawer.Portal>
    <Drawer.Backdrop />
    <Drawer.Popup>
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} style={{ display: 'contents' }}>
        <Drawer.Header>
          <Drawer.Title>Edit item</Drawer.Title>
          <Drawer.Description>Update the fields below.</Drawer.Description>
        </Drawer.Header>
        <Drawer.Panel>
          {/* form fields */}
        </Drawer.Panel>
        <Drawer.Footer>
          <Drawer.Close render={<Button variant="ghost">Cancel</Button>} />
          <Button type="submit">Save</Button>
        </Drawer.Footer>
      </form>
    </Drawer.Popup>
  </Drawer.Portal>
</Drawer.Root>

avoidWhen.reasoning: Use AlertDialog instead. Drawer can be swiped away too easily, which is inappropriate for critical confirmations that require deliberate acknowledgement.

avoidWhen.scenario: Displaying a small contextual tooltip or popover
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Popover or Tooltip instead. Drawer is too heavy for a small piece of contextual information anchored to an element.

avoidWhen.scenario: Content that requires centred modal focus with no directional anchoring
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Dialog instead. Dialog centres content and provides scale/fade animations better suited for focused modal interactions.

examples.name: form
examples.description: Form drawer with submit and cancel actions
examples.code:
const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);

<Drawer.Root open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen}>
  <Drawer.Trigger render={<Button>Add item</Button>} />
  <Drawer.Portal>
    <Drawer.Backdrop />
    <Drawer.Popup>
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} style={{ display: 'contents' }}>
        <Drawer.Header>
          <Drawer.Title>Add item</Drawer.Title>
          <Drawer.Description>Fill in the details below.</Drawer.Description>
        </Drawer.Header>
        <Drawer.Panel>
          {/* form fields */}
        </Drawer.Panel>
        <Drawer.Footer>
          <Drawer.Close render={<Button variant="ghost">Cancel</Button>} />
          <Button type="submit">Save</Button>
        </Drawer.Footer>
      </form>
    </Drawer.Popup>
  </Drawer.Portal>
</Drawer.Root>
